metadata.title:
HOME co, HIRES ITALIANS


AND. FIRES AMERICANS

pages.raw_text:
working with "niggers" in the near
future.


The wages paid by the company
is $500 a day for eight hours,
which is % cents per day less than
paid by the Washington Water
Power Co.. and so cents less than
paid by the city and local contrac-
tors. The action of the company
in this particular instance Is in
keeping with its other actions ever
snce 1t has secured a foothold In
this city.


Local union men say that the
conditions imposed by the new
company have the endorsement OF
the Spokesman-Review and the In
dependent League of American
Workmen, and they point to this
latest act of the Home people as
fully justifying them in insisting
on a closed shop agreement with
that company or nothing. They
claim that 1t Is up to the indie
pendent league as non-union or
ganization to secure decent WORK
ing conditions for the non-unior
men in the employ of the Empire
Electric Construction Co.
